channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-06-19
item.description:
Our community has seen significant progress across multiple projects recently. In the scienceverse/metacheck project, Lakens resolved a code_check crash and added new functions to load paper databases, enhancing the tool's functionality. Meanwhile, giladfeldman made notable updates to docpluck, including documentation for the pre-push canary hook timing and a feature for tables with quality fixes. Additionally, escicheck was synced with the latest version of effectcheck, and auto-zcurve saw revisions to its validation note with a new citation. Lastly, output-it-forward now has a Chrome Webstore link, making it more accessible to users. These connections between projects demonstrate the collaborative spirit of our community, and we celebrate the progress made by our contributors.

123 commits across 7 active repositories this week.

New releases: metacheck v0.1.0-beta

Dormant repos revived: output-it-forward

Most active:

regcheck35 commits
docpluck31 commits
coarse31 commits
metacheck23 commits
escicheck1 commits

item.title: metacheck: 3 commits today
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck

The `fix/code-check-paperlist` branch received a fix to address a code_check crash and resolve the issue of missing paper_id on paper lists, as stated in the commit message "Fix code_check crash and missing paper_id on paper lists". The `papers_databases` branch was updated with new functions to load paper databases from scienceverse/papers, according to the commit "add functions to load paper databases from scienceverse/papers". Both of these changes were made by Lakens, who worked on these two distinct branches to implement the respective fixes and additions. The work on these branches appears to focus on resolving a specific technical issue and enhancing the functionality related to paper databases. No other branches were mentioned in the provided commit messages, indicating that the work was isolated to `fix/code-check-paperlist` and `papers_databases`.

3 commit(s) today:

  • 8cbef44 [fix/code-check-paperlist] Fix code_check crash and missing paper_id on paper lists — Lakens
  • b7afa6a [papers_databases] add functions to load paper databases from scienceverse/papers — Lakens

View all commits →

item.title: docpluck: 2 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck

The `main` branch was worked on, with a commit that added documentation for the pre-push canary hook timing, specifically noting that it should "never timeout/kill the push". Another commit on the `main` branch introduced a feature for tables, referred to as REQUEST_10, which included "HTTP-flatten enabling quality fixes" and "cross-flavor capture" as part of version 2.4.94. The documentation and table feature commits were the only changes made in this set of updates, both of which were applied to the `main` branch. No other branches were modified in this series of commits. The commits focused on documenting the pre-push hook and enhancing the table feature with quality fixes and capture capabilities.

2 commit(s) today:

  • 83ab6bf [main] docs(deploy): document pre-push canary hook timing — never timeout/kill the push — Gilad Feldman
  • 1158b1b [main] feat(tables): REQUEST_10 — HTTP-flatten enabling quality fixes + cross-flavor capture (v2.4.94) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: coarse: 13 commits today
item.description:
Davidvandijcke/coarse

Work was done on the 'main' and 'dev' branches. On the 'dev' branch, the confirmation email and review page were localized to follow the review language, as stated in the commit message "fix(i18n): localize confirmation email + review page follows review language (#244)". The 'dev' branch also had a fix for deployment, specifically enabling RLS on the schema_migrations bookkeeping table, as per the commit "fix(deploy): enable RLS on schema_migrations bookkeeping table (#243)". Additionally, the 'dev' branch was updated by merging the 'origin/main' into 'sync/reconcile-v1.8.0' and merging the 'release/v1.8.0' pull request. The 'main' branch was updated by merging the 'dev' branch via pull request #245.

13 commit(s) today:

  • 05aad76 [main] Merge pull request #245 from Davidvandijcke/dev — David Van Dijcke
  • 49a72b3 [dev] Merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/main' into sync/reconcile-v1.8.0 — Davidvandijcke
  • 2e86c5f [dev] fix(i18n): localize confirmation email + review page follows review language (#244) — David Van Dijcke
  • 907c629 [dev] Merge pull request #242 from Davidvandijcke/release/v1.8.0 — David Van Dijcke
  • f54b584 [dev] fix(deploy): enable RLS on schema_migrations bookkeeping table (#243) — David Van Dijcke

View all commits →

item.title: regcheck: 6 commits today
item.description:
JamieCummins/regcheck

The staging branch was the focus of the recent work, with a push for redeploy being made. As part of the efforts on the staging branch, the developer was "hunting down osf bug", indicating that bug fixing was a key area of focus. Additionally, the staging branch saw changes to enhance visibility, with logging being specifically "improve[d]". The staging branch was also updated in preparation for a redeploy, as evidenced by the "push for redeploy" commit. The commits suggest that the developer was actively addressing issues and refining the application's behavior on the staging branch.

6 commit(s) today:

  • 89a1470 [staging] push for redeploy — Jamie Cummins
  • baebc81 [staging] hunting down osf bug — Jamie Cummins
  • 2f5eaaa [staging] improve logging — Jamie Cummins

View all commits →

item.title: output-it-forward: activity update
item.description:
giovannacdelsordo/output-it-forward

The `main` branch was updated with a commit that added a Chrome Webstore link for OIF, as stated in the commit message "Add Chrome Webstore link for OIF". No other branches were worked on according to the provided commits. The specific change made was the addition of a link, with no other features or fixes mentioned. The commit message does not specify any other modifications or additions beyond the Chrome Webstore link. There are no other commits to describe beyond this single update to the `main` branch.

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →
